commands.name: kasi-cascade
commands.description: Tier cascade orchestration — Opus plans, Sonnet works, Haiku greps

commands.name: kasi-docs
commands.description: Fetch and cache version-matched official documentation

commands.name: kasi-fix
commands.description: Bug fix mission with conservative discipline

commands.name: kasi-init
commands.description: Initialize project for Kasidit — chains scaffold + docs + review + registers auto-invoke

commands.name: kasi-promote
commands.description: Promote fact / pattern / knowledge from project .kasidit/ (dcenterlite) into global Centerlite hub

commands.name: kasi-pull
commands.description: Pull item from Centerlite hub into local project .kasidit/ (dcenterlite)

commands.name: kasi-review
commands.description: Launch code review mission with Kasidit discipline

commands.name: kasi-scaffold
commands.description: Build .kasidit/ knowledge structure for current project

commands.name: kasi-search
commands.description: Semantic search over .kasidit/knowledge/ using local embeddings

commands.name: kasi-security
commands.description: Security audit mission with checklist-driven scanning

commands.name: kasi-status
commands.description: Show current mission state, counter, and context usage

commands.name: kasi-sync
commands.description: Audit diffs between project .kasidit/ (dcenterlite) and global Centerlite hub

commands.name: kasi-ui
commands.description: UI/CSS fix mission with Override Mode

commands.name: kasi-wiki-sync
commands.description: Sync docs/wiki/ content to github wiki repo (github.com/kasidit-wansudon/kasidit.wiki.git)

agents.name: architect-planner
agents.description: Plan BEFORE code for new features, significant refactors, or changes touching more than 2 files. Returns step-by-step plan, file list, data flow, trade-offs. Does NOT write code.

agents.name: bug-hunter
agents.description: Hunt down root cause of a reported bug. Use when test breaks, endpoint returns wrong data, stack trace appears, or user says "why does X fail". Returns minimal-change fix with reproduction.

agents.name: code-reviewer
agents.description: Multi-dimensional code review with quality gates

agents.name: deep-researcher
agents.description: Deep investigation across docs, source, web for library/API/framework questions. Use when task needs version-matched docs, competitor analysis, or codebase survey before deciding. Returns findings + sources, never writes final code.

agents.name: legacy-specialist
agents.description: Navigate and modify legacy codebases (ZF1, old Rails, Vue 2, etc.) without breaking them

agents.name: migration-specialist
agents.description: Handle schema changes, framework upgrades, and data backfills safely. Specialist on backward-compat + zero-downtime patterns. High-risk work — deserves dedicated context.

agents.name: perf-profiler
agents.description: Find performance bottlenecks — slow queries, N+1, large bundles, wasted renders, memory leaks. Use when user reports slowness, high bill, or before scale event. Returns prioritized findings. Finds; does not optimize unless asked.

agents.name: refactor-surgeon
agents.description: Apply a specific, scoped refactor — extract function, rename, dedupe, split file, inline. Use when user names the refactor explicitly. Does NOT decide whether to refactor, only how. Preserves behavior exactly.

agents.name: security-auditor
agents.description: Security-focused reviewer for vulnerability detection in source code

agents.name: test-writer
agents.description: Write unit or integration tests for a specific function, class, or endpoint. Use when user asks for tests, after a bug fix (regression test), or to backfill coverage. Produces runnable test file + gap notes.
